data.title: AI TRENDS | Cambricon Achieves First Annual Profit Amid Beijing's Push for Local Chips
data.description: Cambricon Technologies has announced its first annual profit, marking a significant milestone for the company. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this achievement comes as Beijing urges China's top artificial intelligence developers to utilize domestically produced chips. This move is in response to limitations placed on Nvidia's products. The Chinese government's encouragement is part of a broader strategy to bolster the local semiconductor industry and reduce reliance on foreign technology. Cambricon's success reflects the growing demand for homegrown solutions in the face of international trade restrictions.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Spot Gold Declines Following Statement from Iran's New Supreme Leader
data.description: Spot gold experienced a short-term decline, with losses expanding to $25, following a statement from Iran's newly appointed Supreme Leader. According to Jin10, the current price of spot gold is reported at $5,172.2 per ounce. The market reaction highlights the sensitivity of precious metals to geopolitical developments, particularly in regions with significant influence on global stability. Investors are closely monitoring the situation for further implications on the commodities market.

data.title: Spot gold briefly fell by as much as $25 after the announcement of Iran's new Supreme Leader's statement.
data.description: According to Gate data, spot gold fell by as much as $25 after the announcement of Iran's new Supreme Leader, and is currently trading at $5,172.2 per ounce.

data.title: Iran's New Supreme Leader Calls for National Unity Amid Challenges
data.description: Iran's newly appointed Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, emphasized the importance of unity among individuals and groups within the nation, particularly during challenging times. According to Jin10, Khamenei stated that achieving this unity requires overlooking points of disagreement. His remarks highlight the need for cohesion as the country navigates through its current difficulties.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Microsoft Introduces Health Assistant in Copilot Chatbot
data.description: Microsoft has announced the integration of a dedicated health assistant into its Copilot chatbot, marking a significant step in the company's expansion into AI-driven healthcare solutions. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing trend among technology firms to leverage artificial intelligence for medical services. This move aligns Microsoft with other tech giants who are increasingly investing in AI tools to enhance healthcare delivery and patient engagement. The health assistant aims to provide users with personalized medical advice and support, reflecting the industry's shift towards digital health innovations. Microsoft's initiative underscores the potential of AI in transforming healthcare, offering new opportunities for patient care and management.

data.title: Sanlam Warns of Potential Impact from Middle East Conflict
data.description: Sanlam, Africa's largest insurer, has expressed concerns over the prolonged conflict in the Middle East, highlighting the potential for significant effects on its operations. Bloomberg posted on X that the company emphasized the substantial risks posed by the ongoing situation, which could influence its financial performance. Sanlam's cautionary stance underscores the broader economic implications of geopolitical tensions in the region.

data.title: HM Revenue and Customs (HMRC) plans to introduce encrypted forensics tools to enhance tax compliance for digital assets.
data.description: HMRC (Her Majesty's Revenue and Customs) is exploring the procurement of blockchain forensic software and investigation support services to strengthen its fight against money laundering and tax fraud involving digital assets. A market research announcement released this week indicates that HMRC plans to procure licensing and investigation support services covering blockchain data retrieval, cross-chain transaction tracking, attribution analysis, risk scoring, and data visualization. The proposed contract is worth approximately £3.42 million (about $4.6 million) for three years (April 1, 2027 to March 31, 2029), with an extension until March 2030. The contract includes 55 and 20 software licenses annually, corresponding investigation support hours, and investigator training. Colby Mangels, former OECD consultant and Head of Global Government Solutions at Taxbit, stated that this move reflects the global emphasis on building digital asset compliance infrastructure. Investigations increasingly rely on cross-chain tracking, forensic analysis, and real-time blockchain data access, demonstrating the UK's commitment to setting an example in crypto compliance and regulatory transparency. (The Block)

data.title: Trump: Rising oil prices will make the U.S. a lot of money; the important thing is to prevent Iran from acquiring nuclear weapons.
data.description: US President Trump stated that the US is the world's largest oil producer, far ahead of the competition, so when oil prices rise, we make a lot of money. But for me as president, what's more important is stopping an evil empire—Iran—from acquiring nuclear weapons, and stopping Iran from destroying the Middle East, and even the entire world. I will never let that happen! (Jinshi)

data.title: Peter Thiel Maintained Long-Term Communication with Jeffrey Epstein
data.description: Billionaire tech entrepreneur Peter Thiel maintained a long-term communication with Jeffrey Epstein, focusing on financial matters, political connections, and discussions about the role of deception in evolution. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the nature of their interactions over the years. The relationship between Thiel and Epstein underscores the complex web of connections among influential figures in the tech and political spheres. Despite Epstein's controversial reputation, Thiel's engagement with him persisted, reflecting the intricate dynamics of power and influence.

data.title: AI Data Center Expansion Faces Potential Slowdown Amid Rising Debts
data.description: Technology firms have invested billions in the rapid growth of data centers to support artificial intelligence. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns from Voya Financial's investment division that this demand may soon level off, potentially before the incurred debts are fully repaid. The expansion, driven by the increasing need for AI capabilities, has led to significant borrowing by these companies. However, the anticipated plateau in demand could pose financial challenges as companies work to manage their outstanding obligations.

data.title: Nigeria Halts Gasoline Import Permits Amid Dangote's Influence
data.description: Nigeria has suspended the issuance of import permits for gasoline, aligning with the interests of Aliko Dangote, a prominent business figure. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of this decision on the country's fuel market. The move is seen as a strategic effort to bolster local production and reduce dependency on foreign imports. Dangote, who has been advocating for increased domestic refining capacity, is likely to benefit from this policy shift. The suspension aims to encourage the utilization of Dangote's refinery, which is expected to significantly contribute to Nigeria's energy sector. This development marks a pivotal moment in Nigeria's approach to managing its fuel resources, with potential implications for the broader economy.

data.title: Concerns Rise Over Shadow Lending Following UK Mortgage Lender Collapse
data.description: The recent collapse of MFS, a UK mortgage lender accused of extensive fraud, has heightened worries about the increasing issue in finance related to shadow lending. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the implications of moving lending activities away from regulated banking systems into less transparent areas. This situation underscores the risks associated with financial activities that occur outside traditional banking oversight, raising questions about the stability and security of such practices. The incident serves as a reminder of the potential dangers lurking in the shadow banking sector, where regulation is minimal and oversight is often lacking.

data.title: Cryptio Secures $45 Million in Series B Funding Amid Blockchain Expansion
data.description: Cryptio has successfully raised $45 million in a Series B funding round, highlighting the growing adoption of blockchain-based assets by financial institutions and corporations. According to NS3.AI, the company offers accounting software designed to track digital assets across various platforms, including wallets, custodians, and exchanges. Cryptio currently serves over 450 clients, among them Circle Internet and Société Générale's blockchain subsidiary. This latest funding round follows a $15 million Series A extension completed in January of the previous year.

data.title: Lovable, the Vibe Coding startup, surpasses $400 million in annual recurring revenue.
data.description: Swedish startup Lovable has reached $400 million in annual recurring revenue, with its business growing rapidly as businesses and individual users flock to its AI tools that make programming easy even for beginners. Lovable CEO Anton Osika stated that this progress is five months ahead of initial projections. Founded in 2023, Lovable is part of a group of Vibe Coding startups that allow users to quickly prototype applications and websites using natural language prompts.

data.title: Brazil to Announce Measures to Mitigate Diesel Price Impact from Oil Volatility
data.description: The Brazilian government is set to announce a series of measures aimed at reducing the impact of international oil price fluctuations on diesel prices in the country. According to Jin10, the announcement is scheduled for 10:40 AM local time. The measures are expected to address the challenges posed by volatile oil markets and their effect on domestic fuel prices. The government's initiative reflects its commitment to stabilizing the diesel market and protecting consumers from price surges.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Swedish Startup Lovable Achieves $400 Million Annual Revenue Amid AI Tool Surge
data.description: Swedish startup Lovable has reached an annual recurring revenue of $400 million, driven by the increasing demand for AI tools that simplify programming for beginners. According to Jin10, the company's business has rapidly expanded as both corporate and individual users flock to these innovative solutions. Lovable's CEO, Anton Osika, noted that this milestone was achieved five months ahead of initial projections. Founded in 2023, Lovable is part of a group of Vibe Coding startups that enable users to quickly prototype applications and websites using natural language prompts.

data.title: Concerns Over Speed of Emergency Stockpile Release Amid Supply Gap
data.description: There is growing skepticism about whether the release of emergency stockpiles can occur quickly enough to address the significant supply shortfall. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges faced in bridging this gap. The urgency of the situation underscores the need for efficient and timely measures to mitigate the impact of the supply disruption.

data.title: Binance Wins Second U.S. Anti-Terrorism Act Case as Alabama Court Dismisses Lawsuit
data.description: Key takeawaysBinance secured its second U.S. legal victory under the Anti-Terrorism Act (ATA) within two weeks.A U.S. federal court in Alabama dismissed all claims alleging Binance violated the ATA.The ruling follows a similar decision by a court in the Southern District of New York, which also dismissed ATA claims against Binance.Judges in both cases found the allegations lacked sufficient legal and factual support.Alabama Federal Court Dismisses ATA Claims Against BinanceA U.S. federal court in Alabama has dismissed all claims against Binance in a lawsuit filed under the Anti‑Terrorism Act, marking the exchange’s second major legal victory in the United States in recent weeks.The lawsuit alleged that Binance had violated the ATA through actions related to terrorism financing. However, the court ruled that the complaint failed to meet the required legal standards, effectively dismissing the claims against the crypto exchange.Judge Finds Complaint Legally DeficientIn a 19-page decision, the Alabama judge described the plaintiffs’ filing as a “shotgun pleading,” meaning it lacked clear and specific allegations.According to the ruling:The complaint failed to distinguish between different defendants.Plaintiffs did not provide a clear statement of specific claims or conduct.The case did not meet the required legal threshold for proceeding.The court allowed plaintiffs until April 10, 2026, to file an amended complaint addressing the deficiencies. If the issues are not resolved, the case may be dismissed permanently.Second Court Rejects Similar ClaimsThe decision comes shortly after another major ruling involving Binance.Earlier this month, a federal court in the Southern District of New York dismissed ATA claims brought by 535 plaintiffs who alleged the exchange provided material support linked to 64 terrorist attacks.In that 62-page ruling, the court concluded that the plaintiffs failed to establish any evidence that Binance:Assisted terroristsParticipated in or supported terrorist attacksConspired with terrorist organizationsTogether, the two rulings reinforce that courts reviewing these allegations found them insufficient on both legal and factual grounds.Binance Responds to the RulingsBinance General Counsel Eleanor Hughes said the decisions highlight the importance of due process in cases involving serious allegations.She stated that terrorism financing and sanctions compliance claims require clear evidence and legal rigor, adding that the court outcomes demonstrate that the accusations lacked merit.Compliance Remains Key Focus for BinanceBinance said it will continue investing in compliance infrastructure, regulatory cooperation, and legal governance.The exchange noted that the rulings underscore the importance of fact-based scrutiny in cases involving financial crime and sanctions compliance, particularly within the rapidly evolving cryptocurrency industry.

data.title: U.S. Residential Construction Sees Continued Growth Driven by Multi-Family Projects
data.description: U.S. residential construction has experienced growth for the third consecutive month, reaching its highest pace in nearly a year. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significant contribution of multi-family projects to this upward trend. The construction sector's improvement reflects a robust demand for housing, particularly in urban areas where multi-family developments are increasingly popular. This growth is seen as a positive indicator for the housing market, suggesting sustained interest and investment in residential properties. Analysts are closely monitoring these developments, as they could have broader implications for the economy, including potential impacts on employment and consumer spending. The construction industry's performance is a key factor in economic forecasts, and its recent momentum is viewed as a promising sign for future growth.

data.title: X Layer launches developer incentive program with an initial total prize pool of 200,000 USDT.
data.description: According to official news, X Layer has announced the launch of a developer incentive program with an initial total prize pool of 200,000 USDT to encourage teams to build next-generation smart economic systems on X Layer. Participating projects must be developed based on the X Layer ecosystem, complete at least one transaction on-chain and submit the transaction hash as proof, and open-source the code on GitHub. Integration with x402 payment or use of Onchain OS will receive additional points. The first phase of the initial program runs from March 12th to March 26th, with a prize pool of 50,000 USDT. Judging will focus on the depth of on-chain integration of the AI ​​Agent, the payment process, and its overall impact on the X Layer ecosystem.
